codeReview.summary: Reviewed the accepted feature state via the worker handoff for commit 4ec573293e1441b0e08913101a4bcffbfcdd64c2, the follow-up proof commit 352c3689edffa33856f47a66796545341f48b7dc, transcript skeleton, and source. The implementation now satisfies the feature contract: derive parsing accepts the approved type/field crypto attributes, production runtime code merges generated metadata with process defaults in build_context_for_metadata before auto-registration, and focused runtime/store tests prove those defaults are consumed on first use and remain isolated per model initialization.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The rust-library-worker skill still centers a fail-first edit flow, but this feature was completed through verification/reacceptance because the code was already present; the worker transcript shows a justified verification-only path that the skill only mentions indirectly.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: C:\Users\admin\appdb\.factory\skills\rust-library-worker\SKILL.md steps 4-6 prescribe failing tests then implementation, while worker session 1d56d227-66d2-4856-9666-8a3e3dd3e0b6 reports no edits and handoff.skillFeedback asks for explicit verification-only guidance.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared sensitive-crypto library note documents override precedence but does not explicitly call out the important implementation detail that SECURE_FIELDS may store only explicit overrides and that effective defaults are merged later by the runtime registration path.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: C:\Users\admin\appdb\.factory\library\sensitive-crypto.md lists precedence rules; production behavior is actually realized in C:\Users\admin\appdb\core\src\crypto.rs:250-268 where register_model_crypto_fields/build_context_for_metadata combine metadata with default_crypto_config().

summary: Pass. I reviewed the feature handoff/transcript plus source and diff evidence, including the later proof commit 352c3689edffa33856f47a66796545341f48b7dc. The feature contract is met in the accepted repository state: approved crypto attributes compile, process-wide defaults are consumed by production runtime auto-registration, and focused tests prove first-use default consumption and isolation behavior.
